persons.arguments_against: The surname is not an exact match (bio MOSSEY vs stint MOSSE, fuzzy distance 1), there is no printed birth year, and no edition co-occurrence; one could argue MOSSE and MOSSEY are different families. This fails: every independent dimension agrees. Initials C. B. match; the honour C. B. is shared and appears in every staff record (bio 'C.B. (1874)'); the office matches verbatim including its distinctive dual form — bio 'Deputy Surgeon-General' and 'superintending medical officer for Jamaica, June, 1876' against the staff list's 'Superintending Medical Officer, Deputy Surgeon-General' (Medical Department); colony Jamaica agrees; and the dates are consistent (bio 'retired 1892', stint runs 1878-1890). Only one Superintending Medical Officer / Deputy Surgeon-General of Jamaica held the C.B.; the Mosse/Mossey spelling is ordinary OCR variation.

evidence_cited.fact: superintending medical officer for Jamaica, June, 1876

persons.would_overturn: Evidence that MOSSE and MOSSEY were two distinct C.B. medical officers in Jamaica, not one OCR-variant name.

notes: Shared honour C.B. plus the verbatim dual senior title 'Superintending Medical Officer, Deputy Surgeon-General' in the same colony with consistent dates; surname differs only by an OCR letter. Certain.
